mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier discounting and liquidity conditions to equities, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate is in a sustained downtrend.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive on average during FEDFUNDS downtrends, or if triggered periods do not outperform non-triggered periods by at least 1 percentage point over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising above its recent trend, real rates and policy-tightening expectations increase, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-breakout periods do not show lower 20-day SPX returns than baseline, or if the sign is positive in more than 55% of breakout instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which reduces cyclicals' earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment shock periods fail to produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns stays below 60% across any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains in PAYEMS combined with a falling UNRATE identify broad labor demand strength, which supports earnings breadth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS expansion and UNRATE declines do not outperform single-series labor signals, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in a majority of joint-strength observations over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending, which improves near-term revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if upward RSAFS momentum does not coincide with positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign accuracy falls below 55% in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST indicates housing weakness that spills into employment-sensitive consumption and construction activity, depressing S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if housing downside triggers are not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if negative-return frequency is not at least 60% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL is stable or falling, real activity is improving without inflation pressure, which supports margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if combined output-strength and low-inflation episodes fail to outperform either series alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of joint observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate tightening regime, elevated FEDFUNDS combined with rising CPIAUCSL signals restrictive policy lag and higher real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate inflation episodes do not produce immediate negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate for negative returns is below 60% in any tightening-cycle sample.
